steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Identify the three payment components: a one-time down payment of \$46, $p$ monthly payments of \$21 each (total $21p$), and a grand total of \$214.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Form the equation: down payment + monthly payments = total. Substituting: $46 + 21p = 214$, which is the same as $21p + 46 = 214$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Verify by solving: $21p = 168$, so $p = 8$ monthly payments, and $8 \times 21 + 46 = 168 + 46 = 214$. Correct.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Evaluate every choice:
  • A: $21p - 46 = 214$ — subtracts the down payment instead of adding it; it would imply $p \approx 12.4$, not the intended payment setup. Eliminated.
  • B: $46p - 21 = 214$ — swaps the roles of 46 and 21; it would make $p \approx 5.1$. Eliminated.
  • C: $21p + 46 = 214$ — matches the derived equation exactly. That confirms C is correct.
  • D: $46p + 21 = 214$ — treats 46 as the per-payment amount and 21 as the down payment, reversing both values. Eliminated.
